questions.question: Does InviteMemberModal's card-handling path genuinely discard the profile field from parseContactCard's result and never call writeContactEntry/importCard, or is there a leftover/conditional call path that would write to contactCache under some circumstance (e.g. a debug branch, or reuse of an AddContactModal helper that also caches)?

Does InviteMemberModal reuse the S4-established card-aware scan seam (NpubQrScanner / NpubQrModal onScan) without rebuilding a second QR-parsing path, and does inviteByNpub remain unchanged (npub/pubkey-only), with card-to-pubkey down-conversion happening only at the InviteMemberModal caller, per architecture.md's 'inviteByNpub stays npub/pubkey-only' rule?

Could `group invite input` be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside its `dependencies_allowed` list? If not, which cross-module dependencies exist and do they flow through declared seam contracts in architecture.md?

Is there a test asserting that inviting via a card (both paste and QR-scan) results in zero contactCache writes and zero contacts/knownPeers entries created before the MLS commit lands, driving the real handleInvite/onScan code and asserting on real cache/contacts state, rather than asserting only that a mocked inviteByNpub was called with the right pubkey?

Does the group-invite-by-card path match spec.md / DD 8's description that the invitee's name arrives via MLS profile sync after joining, with no precache shortcut or early contactCache write introduced by this story?

Is the parsed card's profile field (nickname) provably discarded at both the paste and QR-scan call sites — never persisted, never leaked into a UI element that implies contact-cache state, and never passed into any function that would write contacts/knownPeers?

Does supplying a card (link, QR, or payload) to the group invite-by-npub input down-convert it to the card's pubkeyHex via parseContactCard and invite through the existing inviteByNpub path, while writing no card name to contactCache and creating no contacts/knownPeers entry for the invitee before the invite's MLS commit lands? Show the down-conversion production code, a test exercising a real card input through the real invite path, and confirm the test asserts the absence of any cache/contacts write rather than only the presence of the invite call.

Does group-invite-by-card leave the KeyPackage dependency unchanged — does an invitee with no published KeyPackage fail exactly as invite-by-npub does today (the card does not substitute for a KeyPackage)? Show a test that supplies a valid card for a pubkey with no published KeyPackage and asserts the same failure mode as the existing bare-npub-no-KeyPackage case, using the real inviteByNpub path rather than a mocked failure.

VQ-S5-008 asked for the real inviteByNpub path; grepping app/tests/unit at implementation time found no pre-existing unit-test seam for MarmotContext.inviteByNpub's no_key_package branch, and MarmotContext.tsx is outside this story's file scope (sole production file in scope is InviteMemberModal.tsx). The implementation substitutes a PARITY test instead (app/tests/unit/cards/inviteByCard.test.ts, 'AC-GRP-2, parity test' describe block): a stub inviteByNpub whose internal rule mirrors MarmotContext.tsx's actual decision (KeyPackage-set membership -> no_key_package, same shape as its fetchEventsWithTimeout-empty-array check) is invoked once via submitInvite with a bare npub and once via submitInvite with a card for the same pubkey, asserting both calls produce an identical (groupId, npub) argument pair and an identical result. Is this parity substitution an acceptable reading of AC-GRP-2's intent given the file-scope constraint, or does AC-GRP-2 require a follow-up story/finding to add a real MarmotContext-level inviteByNpub no_key_package unit test (which would need to touch MarmotContext.tsx and is therefore out of S5's scope)?

resolveInviteTarget/submitInvite are exported from InviteMemberModal.tsx alongside the default component export, following the exact processContactInput (S4/AddContactModal.tsx) and fireAutoCommit (MarmotContext.tsx) precedent of exporting a pure/async core from an otherwise-component file for jsdom-free unit testing. Does this repo's convention treat that as the settled pattern for testable extraction from component files, or should InviteMemberModal.tsx's two new exports be flagged for a future move into a dedicated non-component module (e.g. alongside contactCard.ts) given group invite input's dependencies_forbidden already excludes it from owning contactCard/contactCardImport concerns?

handleInvite's onScan handler is untouched (still just setNpubInput(scannedNpub); setError(null); scanDisclosure.onClose()) — resolveInviteTarget/submitInvite run only at Submit-button/Enter time inside handleInvite, matching the story's 'single parse point' requirement. Is there any UX expectation (not covered by an AC) that a scanned card should surface its down-converted pubkey or discarded-name state to the user before they press Submit, or is silent pre-fill + parse-on-submit (identical to S4's AddContactModal scan UX) the correct precedent to follow here too?
